new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated for specific 1D models and two physical platforms; the mechanism appears general but extension to higher dimensions, different nonlinearity types, or discrete systems beyond waveguide arrays is not explored

title: Pareto-Efficient Multi-Buyer Mechanisms: Characterization, Fairness and Welfare

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Bayesian mechanism design, Lagrangian duality, cooperative bargaining theory, and distributional analysis (MHR, regularity, revenue curves)
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The sharp divergence between KS (≥50%) and Nash (→0) under MHR is unexpected; the regular ∩ anti-MHR sweet spot is a nice structural surprise, though the general 50% bound for KS follows known bilateral trade techniques

new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims are supported by formal proofs with matching upper and lower bounds in most cases; the unified framework is convincing; minor gaps exist in multi-unit bounds
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are restricted to i.i.d. buyers, single-item/multi-unit homogeneous goods, and specific distributional classes; extension to heterogeneous settings or combinatorial auctions is unclear

title: Adaptive Multi-Head Finite-State Gamblers

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with algorithmic randomness, Kolmogorov complexity, finite-state dimensions, and multi-head automata theory.

title: NFTDELTA: Detecting Permission Control Vulnerabilities in NFT Contracts through Multi-View Learning

title: Cascade: Composing Software-Hardware Attack Gadgets for Adversarial Threat Amplification in Compound AI Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires understanding across three distinct domains: adversarial ML, software vulnerability exploitation, and hardware side-channels/fault injection, which is unusual breadth

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The conceptual framework generalizes well across pipeline architectures, but experiments are limited to specific Llama models and a single benchmark, with hardware attacks only simulated

title: Bayesian policy gradient and actor-critic algorithms

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires strong background in Gaussian processes, Bayesian inference, and policy gradient methods; the proofs involve non-trivial manipulations of Fisher information matrices and kernel functions
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The improved sample efficiency of BQ over MC is expected from prior BQ literature; the main contribution is showing this can be made tractable for policy gradients via Fisher kernels
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are fully specified with pseudocode, experimental details are thorough, and code is stated to be available; however, some implementation details for Fisher information estimation require careful attention
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The GP-based approach does not scale well to the high-dimensional problems common in modern RL applications, limiting practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Experiments are thorough within their scope but limited to small-scale problems; no comparison with stronger baselines; error bars provided but statistical significance not formally tested
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework applies to MDPs and POMDPs but is constrained by Fisher kernel choice and GP scalability; BPG degrades with variable-length trajectories; untested on high-dimensional continuous control

title: Quantum Routing Beyond Pathfinding: Multipartite Entanglement Complementation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with graph state formalism, local complementation rules, and quantum network architecture concepts at the graduate level
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The graph complementation idea is creative but the performance gains (hop reduction) follow somewhat directly from the construction; the NP-completeness bypass is more surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are fully specified with pseudocode, synthetic graph generation is described, and OpenFlights data is public; however, some simulation details and parameter choices are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework is theoretically promising but practical deployment requires scalable multipartite state generation that remains experimentally challenging
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Hop reduction is demonstrated but trivially expected; throughput analysis is parametric without concrete instantiation; scalability claims rest on 50-node networks; preparation overhead is unquantified
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The framework applies to any inter-domain quantum network in principle, but the controlled inter-QNet structure imposes specific architectural requirements that may not suit all deployments

title: Meron Spin Textures Mediated by Acoustic Phase Singularities

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of acoustic metamaterials, topological textures, and spin angular momentum concepts in classical wave systems
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The existence of acoustic spin meron lattices is conceptually expected given analogous optical results; the specific phase-singularity mechanism provides some insight but follows established patterns
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Metasurface parameters and experimental setup are specified; COMSOL simulations are described; but detailed fabrication tolerances, probe calibration, and raw data processing steps are not fully provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Operating at macroscopic scales (~2 kHz) limits direct device applications; potential for acoustic manipulation or sensing remains speculative
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Simulation-experiment agreement is shown qualitatively but lacks error bars, statistical analysis, and systematic parameter sweeps; only two defect configurations tested
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated at single frequency on one metasurface geometry; extension to other frequencies, geometries, 3D configurations, or different wave systems not explored

title: World Model Failure Classification and Anomaly Detection for Autonomous Inspection

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with conformal prediction, world models, and anomaly detection, but the individual components are well-established and the integration is straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected—world model prediction errors correlate with failure, and combining two detectors provides three-way classification as designed
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and partial data promised on project website; training configurations provided; but small dataset and reliance on specific hardware setup limit full reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses industrial inspection needs with hardware demonstration, but small scale and single-task evaluation limit near-term deployment confidence
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Very small dataset (14 training videos per class), no statistical significance tests, no baseline comparisons, and accuracy claims hold for only one metric at one quantile
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on only one task type (gauge reading) in two environments with a very limited dataset; unclear if findings transfer to other inspection tasks or domains

title: Rethinking Intrinsic Dimension Estimation in Neural Representations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with fractal geometry and ID estimation, but the core arguments are accessible to researchers in deep learning theory.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that all reported layer-wise ID patterns are provably not tracking true IDs is surprising in its implications, even though the underlying mathematical fact (dimension non-increase under Lipschitz maps) is classical.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is provided, all models and datasets are publicly available, and experimental details are thorough in the appendix.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Primarily impacts how researchers interpret neural representations; no direct real-world application, but could influence model analysis practices and future estimator design.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Theoretical claims are rigorously proven; empirical evidence spans many architectures consistently. The entropy connection, while compelling, lacks formal theoretical backing.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results apply to any Lipschitz neural network and are demonstrated across CNNs, ViTs, and LLMs, covering vision and language domains with consistent findings.

title: Hybrid Cold-Start Recommender System for Closure Model Selection in Multiphase Flow Simulations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with both recommender systems methodology and multiphase CFD concepts, but the algorithmic components themselves are standard.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that hybrid metadata+collaborative filtering outperforms popularity baselines is expected; the moderate magnitude of improvement is unsurprising given the simple components.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Public simulation setups and evaluation scripts are referenced; gcimpute and scikit-learn implementations are specified. However, the full pipeline integration code and complete data are available only on request.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to CFD practitioners' workflow if the validation database grows; current scope (100 model combinations, bubbly flows only) limits immediate deployment.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Consistent improvements across sparsity levels with confidence intervals and ablation, but absolute margins are modest and the dataset is small (17 experiments). No statistical significance tests beyond CI overlap.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on bubbly flows with 100 out of 500,000+ model combinations; generalization to other flow regimes, broader model spaces, or other scientific domains remains speculative.

title: Explicit Block Encoding of Difference-of-Gaussian Operators on a Periodic Grid

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with quantum block encoding, LCU framework, and spectral analysis of circulant operators
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The O(h⁴) scaling matches existing Laplacian encodings as expected; the probabilistic decomposition is neat but not deeply surprising given the structure of Gaussian kernels
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Circuit construction is fully specified at the abstract level, parameters for the numerical example are given, but no code is provided and Gaussian loader details are delegated to references
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Potentially useful in quantum image processing and PDE pipelines, but practical deployment awaits fault-tolerant hardware and resource benchmarking
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Proofs are correct but numerical validation is minimal—single 1D example with one input function, no resource counts, no comparisons with alternative methods
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The authors hint at extension to any signed combination of normalized distributions over shifts, but only periodic boundaries and Gaussian kernels are treated; multi-dimensional and alternative boundary cases are untested

title: Towards Energy Efficient Co-Scheduling in HPC

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with HPC scheduling, GPU architectures, and NUMA systems, but the techniques themselves (greedy scoring, correlation-based prediction) are straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm intuitive expectations: non-linear scaling means fewer GPUs can save energy, and co-scheduling reclaims idle resources. The magnitude of gains is useful but not surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source release promised, three specific hardware platforms identified, workloads from public benchmarks, but profiling details and hyperparameter sensitivity (e.g., λ, τ) are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to HPC center operations as a SLURM plugin or node-level scheduler module, though the single-node, static-window design needs extension for production use
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Results shown across three platforms with Oracle comparison, but no error bars, limited workload scale, single scheduling window, and no sensitivity analysis for key parameters
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated across three GPU platforms but only 4-GPU nodes, 17 applications, single scheduling windows, and NVIDIA-only tooling; unclear how findings transfer to larger nodes, AMD GPUs, or streaming workloads

title: Probing local coordination and halide miscibility in single-, double-, and triple-halide perovskites using EXAFS

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in EXAFS analysis, wavelet transforms, and synchrotron techniques applied to complex multi-component crystalline materials
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that bromide mediates miscibility and that halides mix at the octahedral level is consistent with prior expectations from bulk measurements and theory; the contribution is more in the confirmation methodology
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Fabrication and measurement procedures are well-described; however, synchrotron beamtime access is required, and some analysis choices (k-range selection, fitting constraints) involve judgment calls that may affect reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Provides design guidelines for triple-halide compositions but uses a model system (MAPbX₃) far from device-relevant mixed-cation compositions
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple complementary techniques support the main claims, but the most novel triple-halide composition required fixed coordination numbers in fitting, and only two triple-halide compositions were tested
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are demonstrated only for MAPbX₃ system; transferability to mixed-cation triple-halide perovskites used in devices remains unverified

title: Egocentric Tactile and Proximity Sensors as Observation Priors for Humanoid Collision Avoidance

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Uses standard RL (PPO) with straightforward sensor abstractions; accessible to graduate students familiar with robot learning
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that sparse non-directional signals outperform dense directional ones is somewhat counterintuitive, though the architecture confound weakens this conclusion
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: General setup is described but many key details (reward weights, PD gains, exact sensor placements) are underspecified; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Idealized sensor assumptions and simulation-only results create a significant gap to real-world sensor design guidance
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple seeds and IQR reported, but no statistical tests, potential architecture confound with ray sensors, and narrow task scope weaken the evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Single task (dodgeball), single robot, idealized sensors, small network—findings may not transfer to richer tasks, architectures, or real sensors

title: Efficient Test-Time Inference via Deterministic Exploration of Truncated Decoding Trees

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The core algorithm is conceptually simple (greedy tree enumeration), requiring only familiarity with truncated sampling and basic tree search. Implementation details are straightforward.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that deterministic exploration outperforms random sampling with replacement is expected; the main contribution is quantifying the gain and providing a practical implementation.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is well-specified with clear pseudocode-level description, standard benchmarks, and named inference engines. Hyperparameters are stated. No code release mentioned but implementation seems straightforward.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable as a drop-in replacement for self-consistency in production LLM inference systems, with demonstrated latency improvements on real serving engines.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Consistent improvements shown across tasks and models, but no error bars/significance tests, missing comparison to sampling-without-replacement baselines, and gains diminish substantially for larger models.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated across math/code/reasoning and multiple models, but effectiveness depends on sharp distributions and constrained tasks; unclear benefit for open-ended generation or frontier-scale models.

title: An Efficient Heterogeneous Co-Design for Fine-Tuning on a Single GPU

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of GPU/CPU memory hierarchies, CUDA streams, and LLM training internals, but builds on well-established concepts
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are impressive in magnitude but directionally expected — overlapping computation with offloading is a known strategy; the gains come from careful engineering
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No code released; while high-level design is clear, many critical implementation details (thread synchronization, cache queue sizing, kernel specifics) would be difficult to reproduce
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to individual researchers and small labs wanting to fine-tune LLMs on consumer hardware; built on PyTorch/Transformers for easy adoption
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Strong throughput and memory benchmarks across multiple models/GPUs, but critical absence of model quality/convergence validation and reliance on synthetic data only
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated across multiple model families and GPU vendors, but limited to transformer architectures and single-GPU settings; NVMe results show degradation at scale

title: Ultrafast Electrocatalytic H2 Evolution from Water Achieved by a Cobalt-NHC Catalyst

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in molecular electrochemistry, Savéant framework for catalytic analysis, and PCET mechanisms
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: A two-order-of-magnitude improvement in TOF over the state of the art is highly unexpected and challenges assumptions about the limits of molecular HER catalysis
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Main text describes methodology but critical supplementary information (17+ figures/tables) is unavailable; catalyst synthesis details not provided in main text; no code or raw data shared
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Neutral pH operation with earth-abundant Co is promising for practical electrolysis, but device integration, long-term stability, and ligand synthesis scalability remain unaddressed
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple control experiments and 11 independent runs support the TOF claim, but the diffusion-limited regime complicates interpretation, and supplementary evidence is inaccessible for evaluation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The NHC design principle could extend to other metals and reactions, but results are demonstrated for only one catalyst under specific buffer conditions; broader applicability is suggested but unproven

title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Looped Reasoning Language Models

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Transformer internals, fixed-point theory, and mechanistic interpretability, but the proofs themselves are relatively straightforward.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The cyclic fixed-point behavior is somewhat expected given prior work on deep equilibrium models, but the discovery that looped models self-organize into feedforward-like stages of inference is a moderately surprising and insightful finding.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All pretrained models are publicly available on Huggingface, metrics are well-defined, and the small-scale training code is based on a public repository. Some algorithm hyperparameters are manually tuned, but overall replication should be feasible.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Findings on normalization, input injection, and fixed-point stability provide actionable design guidance for looped LLM architectures, but no new models or direct performance improvements are demonstrated.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Extensive empirical validation across multiple models, metrics, and datasets with thorough appendix. However, small-scale training experiments use only 3 seeds, and some claims about causality (e.g., normalization causing lack of stages) are correlational.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are demonstrated across several architectures and two datasets, but all models are relatively small (≤3.5B), only cyclic recurrence is studied, and small-scale experiments raise questions about scaling.

title: NLiPsCalib: An Efficient Calibration Framework for High-Fidelity 3D Reconstruction of Curved Visuotactile Sensors

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with photometric stereo, variational optimization, and visuotactile sensor design, but the mathematical framework is adopted from prior work
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that near-light photometric stereo fits visuotactile sensor optics is reasonable and somewhat expected once stated; the results confirm known physics rather than revealing surprises
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Sensor fabrication details and NLiPs formulation are well-described; however, specific optimization hyperparameters, LED calibration procedures, and code are not fully provided despite a project webpage
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to custom visuotactile sensor development, though requires adopting the specific controllable-LED hardware design
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Limited test cases (5 objects), no statistical reporting, no direct comparison with CNC-based calibration on the same sensor, and no downstream task evaluation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated across three elastomer geometries which is encouraging, but restricted to Lambertian coatings with controllable LEDs; unclear how it transfers to different coating materials or sensor architectures

title: Coherence dynamics in Simon's quantum algorithm

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The calculations require basic familiarity with quantum computing and coherence measures but involve only straightforward algebraic manipulations.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results — coherence increases with system size, unitaries preserve global coherence — are entirely expected from the structure of the states involved.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All calculations are explicitly shown and could be easily reproduced by anyone familiar with the coherence measures and Simon's algorithm.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The results are purely descriptive calculations with no pathway to practical application in algorithm design or quantum hardware.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: The algebraic calculations support the stated theorems, but the claims about broader significance ('shedding new light') are unsupported.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The results are specific to Simon's algorithm and the chosen coherence measures, with no framework for extension to other settings.

title: MAED: Mathematical Activation Error Detection for Mitigating Physical Fault Attacks in DNN Inference

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with fault injection attacks, DNN implementations on embedded platforms, and numerical methods, but the mathematical concepts are straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The approach is a direct transfer of algorithm-level error detection from cryptography to DNN activation functions; results are expected given the methodology
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code available, simulation parameters clearly specified, and implementations described on specific hardware platforms
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to embedded AI systems using Taylor-series activation functions, but limited to a narrow implementation paradigm
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Thorough fault simulation but narrow fault target assumptions, no end-to-end misclassification prevention demonstration, and detection rates below claimed levels in several scenarios
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to Taylor-series implementations of three activation functions on specific platforms; does not address quantized networks, lookup-table implementations, or modern accelerator architectures

title: Complexity-Aware Theory Testing from Bell Witnesses

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with information theory, Bell inequalities, and model selection, but the mathematical techniques used are standard.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected given the known data processing inequality and existing KL-based Bell analysis; the quantitative conservativeness ratios are mildly informative but not surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The Wang et al. data table is reproduced, model specifications are explicit, and the mathematical framework is fully specified; simulation details could be more complete.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could provide practical guidance for Bell experimentalists on model selection, but the conservative nature of the bound and BIC limitations reduce immediate applicability.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims are supported by theory, bootstrap, simulation, and multiple complexity proxies, but the single primary dataset and acknowledged BIC limitations weaken the empirical evidence.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The theoretical framework extends to any binary Bell game (demonstrated with GHZ), but the detailed empirical analysis is limited to CHSH and a single dataset.

title: Specification-Aware Distribution Shaping for Robotics Foundation Models

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with STL semantics, constrained optimization, reinforcement learning, and robotics foundation models — graduate-level knowledge across multiple subfields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm expected behavior: STL constraints are satisfied at the cost of main task performance. The closed-form solution follows straightforwardly from standard KL-constrained optimization
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The algorithm is well-specified and the simulator is publicly available, but key details about DQN training, funnel parameters, and occupancy map construction are underspecified; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The concept is practically motivated but strong assumptions (deterministic dynamics, known layout, discrete actions, simulation-only) limit near-term real-world deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only two case studies with no baselines beyond unmodified SPOC, no ablations, no confidence intervals, and no analysis of failure modes or computational overhead
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Restricted to discrete action spaces, deterministic dynamics, known structural layouts, and navigation-only tasks in a single simulator with one foundation model

title: ALPHA-PIM: Analysis of Linear Algebraic Processing for High-Performance Graph Applications on a Real Processing-In-Memory System

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with PIM architectures, sparse linear algebra, and graph processing frameworks, but the implementations use existing tools and concepts
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely confirm expected PIM characteristics—data transfer bottlenecks, low compute utilization, GPU outperformance—with few unexpected findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: UPMEM hardware is commercially available, datasets are public, and implementation details are provided, but no code release is mentioned and UPMEM access is limited
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Findings inform future PIM hardware design but current UPMEM system does not outperform GPUs, limiting immediate practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Thorough execution breakdowns and profiling, but weak baselines undermine comparative claims and limited statistical analysis (no error bars, variance reporting)
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Findings are heavily tied to UPMEM's specific architecture; unclear how results transfer to other PIM designs, larger graphs, or non-traversal algorithms

title: SoK: Practical Aspects of Releasing Differentially Private Graphs

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of differential privacy, graph theory, and privacy attacks, but the systemization framework itself is designed to be accessible to practitioners.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that edge-DP methods uniformly fail against structural correlation attacks and that node-DP methods (Jian et al.) are effectively unusable is somewhat surprising and practically important, though the underlying independence assumption issue was previously noted theoretically.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code released, detailed experimental setup with specific hyperparameters, datasets are publicly available, and evaluation methodology is thoroughly described.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The practitioner-oriented framework is directly applicable to organizations needing to release graph data privately, though the negative findings about existing methods' limitations somewhat limit immediate deployment value.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive utility evaluation across multiple metrics and datasets, but limited attack evaluation (2-3 attacks), no statistical significance tests, restricted to one domain, and modest dataset scales weaken overall evidence.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluations restricted to four social network datasets; the framework is designed to be general but is only demonstrated in one domain, and the authors explicitly note results may not transfer to other domains.

title: Architecture-Agnostic Feature Synergy for Universal Defense Against Heterogeneous Generative Threats

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with adversarial attacks, generative models, and multi-objective optimization, but the method itself is relatively straightforward.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that feature spaces align across generative architectures is intuitive and not particularly surprising; the results confirm expected benefits of feature-level optimization.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is fully specified with pseudocode, implementation details provided, and code is promised to be open-sourced, but some design choices (target image selection) lack justification.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to facial image protection but limited by white-box assumptions and narrow evaluation scope.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Results show consistent improvements but lack error bars, statistical significance tests on main claims, limited baselines, and the theoretical claims about gradient orthogonality and feature alignment are not rigorously supported.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on facial images with three model families; claims of universality are not substantiated by the experimental scope.

title: Computation of frequency- and time-domain Jacobians in optical tomography with Monte Carlo simulations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of radiative transfer, perturbation MC methods, functional analysis (Fréchet derivatives), and FEM, along with domain knowledge in optical tomography instrumentation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: MC-DA agreement in diffusive regimes, disagreement near CSF, and marginal detector model effects at short separations all align with prior understanding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source MCX implementation with demo scripts and public head model database; however, detector model code is available only upon request and some simulation details (GPU allocation, batch specifics) are loosely specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to optical tomography reconstruction pipelines used in neonatal brain imaging and potentially breast imaging, though computational cost may limit clinical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multi-level validation is thorough but primarily qualitative; lacks quantitative error metrics, convergence analysis, and reconstruction demonstrations that would more convincingly support claims of practical utility
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework applies to any voxelized medium where RTE holds, but validation limited to neonatal head models at single wavelength and single modulation frequency; detector model is instrument-specific

title: VLM-DEWM: Dynamic External World Model for Verifiable and Resilient Vision-Language Planning in Manufacturing

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with VLM architectures, scene graph representations, Bayesian state estimation, and robotic manipulation—spanning multiple subdisciplines at graduate level.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm expected intuitions: explicit state management helps VLMs, structured reasoning beats unstructured context. The magnitude of baseline failures (0% in Task 3) is somewhat surprising but partly reflects task design.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Extensive appendices with algorithms, prompts, and hyperparameters, but no code/data release, heavy reliance on proprietary VLM (Gemini 2.5 Pro), and complex multi-module integration would make reproduction challenging.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets manufacturing deployment with industrially relevant tasks; the semi-structured environment assumption aligns with real factory settings, though system complexity and VLM cost are barriers.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Simulation uses privileged perception (not realistic), real-world has only 60 trials without statistical significance tests, baselines score 0% suggesting potential task design bias, and no error bars are reported.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on one robot platform with semi-closed world assumptions; requires CAD priors and known categories; latency constraints limit applicability to deliberative tasks only.

title: From edges to meaning: Semantic line sketches as a cognitive scaffold for ancient pictograph invention

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with CLIP, differentiable rendering, visual neuroscience concepts, and ancient writing systems, but the technical implementation is relatively straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that CLIP-optimized sketches resemble pictographs is interesting but somewhat expected given that both represent simplified visual abstractions of the same objects
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The framework builds on CLIPasso with publicly available models and datasets, though some implementation details (sketchability classifier training) are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The proto-cuneiform decipherment application is intriguing but far from validated; practical archaeological utility would require extensive expert validation
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Circular evaluation (CLIP optimizes and evaluates), missing null models for pictograph matching, many categories show near-zero matching, and no ablation against non-semantic baselines
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only 11 categories tested with highly uneven results; scene categories mostly fail; only 2-3 writing systems examined with limited deciphered vocabulary overlap

title: A Survey on Split Learning for LLM Fine-Tuning: Models, Systems, and Privacy Optimizations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The paper is a survey requiring familiarity with split learning, federated learning, LLM fine-tuning, and privacy mechanisms, but does not introduce novel technical concepts requiring deep specialist expertise.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: As a survey paper, it organizes existing knowledge rather than producing surprising findings; the identified bottlenecks and categorizations largely align with community expectations.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Survey paper with no empirical component to reproduce.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The unified pipeline framework could guide practitioners designing split-based LLM fine-tuning systems, though the lack of benchmarking limits direct applicability.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Survey paper with no original empirical claims to evaluate evidence for.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The taxonomy is reasonably general across SL variants, but many surveyed methods lack LLM validation, and the framework's applicability to the largest-scale models or non-Transformer architectures is unclear.

title: Dual-Guard: Dual-Channel Latent Watermarking for Provenance and Tamper Localization in Diffusion Images

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of latent diffusion models, watermarking theory, and forensic methods, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The dual-channel complementarity is intuitive once stated; the strong detection results are expected given the reference-assisted Full mode setup
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture and training details are provided, thresholds are specified, but no code is released and some implementation details require careful reconstruction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to creator platforms with asset registries, though storage requirements and single-model evaluation limit broader deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Good coverage of attack types with confidence intervals, but limited baselines, no adaptive attacks, single model, and the reference-stored setup inflates detection performance
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on SD 2.1-base at 512x512; no evaluation on other diffusion architectures, resolutions, or generation paradigms

title: CSSDF-Net: Safe Motion Planning Based on Neural Implicit Representations of Configuration Space Distance Field

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with configuration space geometry, neural implicit representations, and optimization-based motion planning
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected given prior work on C-space distance fields; the approach works roughly as anticipated with no surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Network architecture and loss functions are specified, but key details about training data size, spatial hashing resolution, and hyperparameters for experiments are incomplete; partial code reference exists
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real robotics need with practical inference speeds, but non-zero collision risk and lack of formal safety guarantees limit immediate deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Ablations are limited to small sample sizes, the most relevant baseline (CDF) is missing, collision risk is non-zero but not analyzed, and real-world experiments lack quantitative metrics
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on simple obstacle configurations with two robot platforms; generalization to complex scenes, different robot morphologies, and dense environments is unvalidated

title: Wasserstein Formulation of Reinforcement Learning. An Optimal Transport Perspective on Policy Optimization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep familiarity with optimal transport theory, Riemannian geometry on Wasserstein spaces, ergodic theory of Markov chains, and reinforcement learning theory simultaneously
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The gradient recovers the known ∇Q result; the Hessian decomposition is structurally interesting but its practical implications remain unexplored, and the convexity result is limited to a trivial case
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm pseudocode is provided and environments are described in detail, but no code is released, hyperparameter sensitivity is not discussed, and some implementation choices are ambiguous
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The theoretical framework is elegant but the practical algorithms reduce to known approaches; the Hessian has not been exploited algorithmically, limiting near-term applicability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Experiments demonstrate basic functionality but lack baselines, ablations, statistical rigor (only 10 seeds), and fail to show advantages of the Wasserstein perspective over standard methods
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Smoothness assumptions, deterministic policy focus in experiments, and small-scale environments limit generalizability; discrete action spaces and stochastic policies are not addressed

title: COD-ssi: Enforcing Mutual Privacy for Credential Oblivious Disclosure in Self Sovereign Identity

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with OPRFs, AEAD, and SSI credential models, but no deep specialist expertise in any single subdomain
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The problem identification (Verifier privacy gap) is somewhat surprising; the solution approach using OPRFs is natural once the problem is framed, and performance results are as expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source prototype promised, algorithms fully specified with pseudocode, concrete parameter choices documented, though single-machine setup limits reproducibility of exact performance numbers
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Compatible with W3C standards and existing SSI infrastructure, but the malicious Holder vulnerability must be resolved before production deployment in adversarial settings
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Security proofs are standard compositional reductions (solid but not deep), experiments are comprehensive in scaling but limited to single-machine JS benchmarks without network or mobile evaluation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Construction is OPRF-agnostic in principle, but only tested with 2HashDH and hash-based SD; applicability to BBS+, ZKP-based credentials, or post-quantum settings remains theoretical

title: Limited Perfect Monotonical Surrogates constructed using low-cost recursive linkage discovery with guaranteed output

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep familiarity with linkage learning, Walsh decomposition, partition crossover, and gray-box optimization—a specialized subdomain of evolutionary computation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The O(log n) guaranteed linkage discovery is a nice result, but the overall approach extends existing frameworks in a somewhat expected direction; the competitive performance gains are meaningful but not dramatic
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available on GitHub, algorithms are presented in detailed pseudocode, all benchmark problems are well-defined, and experimental setup is precisely specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Designed for expensive real-world optimization but only demonstrated on synthetic benchmarks; single-variable comparison limitation reduces immediate practical applicability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Thorough synthetic benchmarks with statistical tests and 30 repetitions, but absence of real-world problems weakens the evidence for the paper's primary motivation; ablation is partial
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Currently limited to binary/integer combinatorial problems with single-variable comparisons; only synthetic benchmarks tested; extension to continuous or permutation spaces is mentioned but not demonstrated

title: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications for Mitigation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of transformer internals, attention mechanisms, and mechanistic interpretability methods
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The specific circuit components (query bias, positional encoding, key projection) are largely expected given prior work by Sun et al. and Dial; the dispensability argument is the most surprising element
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and data publicly available, methods clearly specified, uses a public model checkpoint, and interventions are well-documented
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The dispensability argument has practical implications for mitigation design, but no concrete mitigation strategies are proposed or tested
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Causal interventions are well-designed with controls, but residual sinks after ablations weaken claims, evaluation is small-scale, and the dispensability argument relies on citing rather than testing other architectures
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Findings are specific to GPT-2's architecture (learned query biases, absolute positional embeddings); the dispensability argument suggests the circuit does NOT generalize, which is the paper's point but limits direct applicability

title: Bridging SFT and RL: Dynamic Policy Optimization for Robust Reasoning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with RL for LLMs, policy gradient methods, and variance reduction techniques, but the individual components are well-known.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm expected behavior—combining SFT and RL with intelligent routing outperforms naive approaches. The magnitude of OOD improvement is somewhat surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available, hyperparameters are specified, base models are open-source, and the training framework (verl) is documented. Multi-teacher data generation details are provided.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to LLM post-training pipelines for mathematical reasoning; framework could be adopted by practitioners training reasoning models.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive benchmarks with ablations, hyperparameter sensitivity, and statistical significance tests. Some concerns about fairness of teacher model comparisons.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to mathematical reasoning with binary rewards; the difficulty grading mechanism doesn't naturally extend to open-ended tasks with continuous or subjective evaluation.

title: Dataset Distillation for Machine Learning Force Field in Phase Transition Regime

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The method involves standard ML tools (PCA, density estimation, weighted sampling) applied to a domain-specific problem; accessible to graduate students in computational chemistry/physics.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that sampling both representative and outlier configurations improves training is intuitive and expected; the degree of improvement over baselines is the modest surprise.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and datasets are publicly available on GitHub, and the method is straightforward enough to reimplement. However, some hyperparameter choices lack detailed justification.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Direct practical application to reducing computational cost of MLFF training with expensive quantum chemistry methods, though not yet demonstrated with beyond-DFT labels.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single system validation, no error bars, no statistical tests, small dataset, and missing comparisons with standard subsampling baselines weaken the evidence substantially.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only validated on one system (hydrogen LLPT at 1000 K); claims about broader applicability to other phase transitions and materials are entirely speculative.

title: Skill Substitution, Expectations, and the Business Cycle

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with labor economics, education economics, and applied econometrics including detrending strategies and decomposition methods
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Procyclical college enrollment directly contradicts the well-established stylized fact; the expectations mechanism showing misaligned beliefs about vocational stability is also unexpected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Administrative data requires formal access through German Research Data Centers; econometric approach is well-specified but exact replication requires navigating institutional data access
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant for education policy design in countries with dual education systems considering how to buffer human capital investment from business cycles
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Main enrollment results are well-supported with extensive robustness checks; expectations mechanism is suggestive but limited to four survey waves with coarse measures
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are tightly linked to Germany's institutional features; author discusses external validity but applicability outside dual-system countries with centralized higher education funding is uncertain

title: Lost in Translation: Simulation-Informed Bayesian Inference Improves Understanding of Molecular Motion From Neutron Scattering

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in neutron scattering theory, Bayesian inference, molecular dynamics simulations, and the mathematical derivation of rotational scattering models — a combination spanning multiple subdisciplines
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The anisotropy ratio of ~11, far exceeding the previously accepted ~2.3, is genuinely surprising and challenges existing understanding of benzene liquid dynamics
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All code, data, and analysis workflows are openly available on GitHub and Zenodo with explicit licenses; simulation parameters and experimental details are thoroughly documented
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework could be applied to industrially relevant catalytic systems and energy materials, though it requires specialized neutron facilities with polarisation analysis capabilities
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Strong Bayesian evidence comparisons and systematic validation on simulated data before experimental application, though the single-system demonstration and unquantified resolution function effects limit confidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The framework is conceptually general but demonstrated only for liquid benzene; applicability to confined systems, larger molecules, or non-symmetric rotors remains to be shown

title: Ultra-Fast 3D Porous Media Generation: a GPU- Accelerated List-Indexed Explicit Time-Stepping QSGS Algorithm

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The core concept (active front tracking) is straightforward and accessible to anyone with basic GPU programming and computational physics background
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The speedup from restricting computation to the active front is entirely expected; the magnitude is the only mildly interesting aspect
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code on GitHub, clear pseudocode, specified hardware, and well-defined parameter settings make reproduction straightforward
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to digital rock physics workflows and could accelerate uncertainty quantification studies in reservoir engineering
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Timing benchmarks lack error bars and statistical rigor; cross-hardware comparisons are not fully controlled; morphological validation is limited to basic size distributions
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on single-phase Fontainebleau sandstone; no demonstration for multiphase systems, carbonates, or other complex porous media types

title: DecoVLN: Decoupling Observation, Reasoning, and Correction for Vision-and-Language Navigation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of VLN, VLMs, imitation learning, and POMDP formulations, but individual components are relatively standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are improvements as expected from the proposed components; no counterintuitive findings emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and project page mentioned; training details provided including hyperparameters, but some aspects like the long-horizon validation set construction details and exact data processing pipelines could be more explicit
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Real-world deployment on quadruped robot demonstrated; RGB-only requirement lowers hardware barriers; device-server architecture is practical though latency-dependent
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive ablations, multiple baselines compared, two benchmarks tested, but lacks statistical significance tests, error bars, and quantitative real-world evaluation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on R2R-CE and RxR-CE; real-world evaluation is qualitative only; no evaluation on other VLN benchmarks or diverse robot platforms

title: Near-Field Integrated Sensing, Computing and Semantic Communication in Digital Twin-Assisted Vehicular Networks

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of MIMO optimization, CRB analysis, semantic communication models, and convex optimization techniques including SDP relaxation and alternating optimization
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: semantic communication improves throughput, particle filters outperform EKF for nonlinear tracking, and the hybrid heuristic outperforms greedy assignment
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Simulation parameters are provided in Table III, but key details like the DT model coefficients, semantic encoder specifics, and exact algorithm implementation details are insufficient for full reproduction; no code provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework targets practical vehicular networks but relies on simplified models (LoS channels, constant velocity, ad-hoc DT computing model) that would require substantial modification for real deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Numerical results cover multiple metrics and baselines, but the DT computing model is unvalidated, the low-SNR approximation tightness is not verified, rank relaxation quality is unreported, and no statistical confidence measures are provided
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are demonstrated only for a specific highway scenario with 2 RSUs and up to 20 vehicles; the DT computing model is ad-hoc and the NF channel model is LoS-only, limiting transferability to other environments

title: ProHunter: A Comprehensive APT Hunting System Based on Whole-System Provenance

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of graph neural networks, provenance analysis, and systems security; the bit-level encoding and inter-graph message passing are technically involved but build on established techniques.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm expected improvements from combining better compression, sampling, and representation; the 100% recall is strong but not fundamentally surprising given the controlled DARPA benchmarks.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available and datasets are standard benchmarks, but semi-automated query graph construction and hand-crafted abstract types/sampling rules introduce reproducibility challenges.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The 3-25 MB/day memory footprint and real-time processing capability make this practically deployable, though the gap between DARPA benchmarks and production environments remains.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive evaluation across 28 scenarios with ablations and comparisons, but Sleuth comparison uses estimates, no statistical significance tests, and DARPA datasets may not represent real-world complexity.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Cross-platform evaluation across four OS types is strong, but hand-crafted abstract types, sampling rules, and evaluation only on DARPA datasets limit confidence in generalization to novel attack types and production environments.

title: Reasoning Graphs: Deterministic Agent Accuracy through Evidence-Centric Chain-of-Thought Feedback

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The concepts involve graph structures and RAG pipelines that are accessible to anyone with graduate-level NLP knowledge; no deep mathematical machinery required
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that persisting and reusing past reasoning should improve consistency is intuitive; the evidence-centric framing is a modest conceptual surprise but results are unknown
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Detailed schema, algorithms, and hyperparameters provided, but no code released and no experiments to reproduce; the framework is implementable from the description
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: If validated, directly applicable to enterprise RAG systems with repeated query patterns over stable knowledge bases
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No experimental results whatsoever; all claims about accuracy convergence, variance collapse, and comparative performance are entirely unvalidated
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is general in principle but depends on passage reuse across queries and reliable outcome signals, limiting applicability to specific deployment patterns

title: Sim-to-Real Transfer for Muscle-Actuated Robots via Generalized Actuator Networks

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with sim-to-real transfer, RL, rigid body dynamics, and actuator modeling, but the concepts are accessible to robotics graduate students.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The success of learned actuator models for sim-to-real is somewhat expected given prior work on quadrupeds; the extension to muscle actuation is valuable but conceptually anticipated.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hyperparameters are thoroughly documented in appendices, architecture details are given, and the pipeline is clearly described, though code and data are not released and the specific robot platform limits reproducibility.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to deploying muscle-actuated robots in real settings; limited by the niche of PAM-actuated systems but the general principle extends to other non-standard actuators.

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Method is designed to generalize across actuator types and validated on one complex platform, but tested on only one robot with two tasks; claims of broad applicability are plausible but unverified.

title: Algorithms of self-synchronizing single-deletion-correcting codes

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with algebraic coding theory, CIS codes, and deletion-correcting codes over finite fields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The conditional complementation trick is somewhat expected given the problem structure; the results don't overturn any conventional understanding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are specified in pseudo-code with examples, but code is not provided, pseudo-code has apparent errors, and key implementation details for larger codes are missing
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: DNA storage is a real application area, but the restrictive error model and lack of practical validation leave significant gaps before real-world deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only hand-worked toy examples on length-6 codes; no simulations, no rate comparisons, no statistical analysis, and pseudo-code contains errors
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to single non-consecutive deletions over GF(4), requires CIS codes with all-one codeword; unclear how to extend to multi-error or mixed-error channels

title: TopRank-Based Delivery Rate Optimization for Coded Caching under Non-Uniform Demands

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with coded caching theory, online learning-to-rank, and concentration inequalities, spanning multiple subfields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The ranking-over-estimation insight is sensible but not deeply surprising; the performance gains in specific scenarios are moderately expected given the algorithm's design
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm pseudocode is provided and MovieLens dataset is public, but key parameters (H, δ choices) lack clear guidance and no code is released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Coded caching is relevant to CDN design, but the gap between the simplified model and real deployment remains significant
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only two experimental scenarios, single baseline, no error bars, no formal regret bounds despite sublinear regret claims in the abstract
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested only with one dataset, two network configurations, and specific cache sizes; unclear how results extend to diverse popularity distributions or network scales

title: HEX: Humanoid-Aligned Experts for Cross-Embodiment Whole-Body Manipulation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in VLA models, humanoid control, MoE architectures, flow matching, and real-world robot systems integration
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm that structured proprioceptive modeling helps humanoid control, which is intuitive; the magnitude of generalization improvement on some tasks is notable but not paradigm-shifting
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom humanoid platforms (Tienkung 2.0/3.0), proprietary teleoperation setup, in-house datasets, and no code release make independent replication extremely difficult
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly demonstrated on full-sized physical humanoid robots performing practical manipulation tasks, with clear path to deployment in industrial and domestic settings

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on two humanoid platforms with diverse tasks and some generalization variants, but all in similar lab environments; cross-embodiment pretraining benefit shown to be primarily optimization-related rather than performance-enhancing

title: Encrypted clones can leak: Classification of informative subsets in Quantum Encrypted Cloning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with quantum information, Pauli algebra, and density matrix formalism, but the computations are relatively standard.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The parity-dependent leakage and the specific role of the y-Bloch component are somewhat unexpected, though the existence of some leakage in non-authorized subsets is not entirely surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All derivations are presented with explicit intermediate steps, making independent verification straightforward. No code or data needed.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Results inform quantum storage architecture design but are specific to a protocol not yet widely deployed; no direct practical implementation is offered.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims are supported by complete algebraic proofs for all cases, though the analysis lacks information-theoretic quantification and excludes subsets containing the source qubit.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are specific to the single-qubit Pauli-based encrypted cloning protocol; extension to higher dimensions or modified protocols is not addressed.

title: Testing Scalar Field Dark Matter models in M31 galaxy through the Rotation Curve analysis

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with galactic dynamics, dark matter models, and nonlinear fitting, but the methods employed are standard.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that cored halos outperform oscillatory ones and that baryonic modeling matters are both expected results consistent with prior literature.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Density profiles and fitting method are specified, and the data source is cited, but no code is provided and best-fit parameter uncertainties are absent, making exact replication difficult.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: This is fundamental astrophysics research with no direct pathway to real-world application.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single galaxy, no parameter uncertainties, no MCMC exploration, and limited statistical measures (only BIC) weaken the evidential support for the claims.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Analysis is limited to a single galaxy (M31); conclusions may not transfer to other galaxy types or mass ranges without further validation.

title: Generalizing Numerical Reasoning in Table Data through Operation Sketches and Self-Supervised Learning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with table QA, program synthesis, and self-supervised learning, but the individual components are straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Header anonymization improving generalization is somewhat expected; the magnitude of sketch-driven improvement and data efficiency gains are moderately surprising, but the sketch dependency tempers this
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hyperparameters and training details are well-specified, but dataset construction relies on GPT-3.5 paraphrasing, and the NumReason-500 dataset availability is unclear

title: When the Agent Is the Adversary: Architectural Requirements for Agentic AI Containment After the April 2026 Frontier Model Escape

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with OS security primitives, AI safety concepts, and systems architecture, but no deep specialist expertise is needed
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The observations and requirements are largely expected given the incident; the agent-as-adversary framing is not new to security researchers
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No implementation, no experiments, no formal models — the requirements are descriptive and the assessments are subjective
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Requirements address real deployment needs but are specified at too high a level to be directly implementable; author's patents may eventually lead to products
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims rest on re-description of public disclosures and subjective assessment of existing systems; no independent analysis, formal proofs, or experimental validation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The threat model and requirements are broadly applicable to agentic AI systems in general, though the specific requirements are untested

title: Grain Growth Kinetics in (Cr,Mo,Ta,V,W)C1-δ High-Entropy Carbide Ceramics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of ceramic processing, sintering kinetics, and high-entropy materials, but the analytical framework is relatively standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: grain growth increases with temperature, Arrhenius behavior is observed, and the activation energy falls within the range of known refractory carbides

title: GenZ-LIO: Generalizable LiDAR-Inertial Odometry Beyond Indoor--Outdoor Boundaries

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of state estimation, Kalman filtering, point cloud processing, and control theory, but builds on well-established frameworks

title: SWAN: World-Aware Adaptive Multimodal Networks for Runtime Variations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with differentiable sorting networks, Gumbel-based relaxations, multimodal fusion architectures, and 3D object detection pipelines
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm intuitive expectations — QoI-aware allocation helps under corruption, and sample-adaptive gating saves compute on easy inputs. The NeuralSort advantage over straight-through is the most surprising finding

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated only on one dataset (nuScenes), one base architecture (CMT), one task (3D detection), and two modalities (LiDAR+camera); claims of broader applicability are unsupported

title: Learning Invariant Modality Representation for Robust Multimodal Learning from a Causal Inference Perspective

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with causal inference, information theory, and multimodal learning, but the individual techniques are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm expected benefits of invariant representation learning; the magnitude of OOD improvement is the main notable outcome
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available, hyperparameters are fully specified, and experimental protocols are detailed in appendices
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to multimodal sentiment analysis systems, though gap between noise-simulated environments and real-world shifts limits immediate deployment confidence
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive experiments with ablations, significance tests, and probing studies, but theoretical-practical gap (orthogonality vs MI, noise vs real environments) weakens some claims

title: Direction Matters: Learning Force Direction Enables Sim-to-Real Contact-Rich Manipulation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of admittance control, contact mechanics, and sim-to-real transfer, but the core concepts are accessible to robotics researchers

title: Mirror Dual Symmetry in Physics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The paper requires only basic familiarity with quantum mechanics and the Dirac equation; no advanced techniques are employed.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The core idea (zero-energy universe, CPT-symmetric sectors) has been proposed before; no new results or predictions are derived.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: There are no calculations, simulations, or derivations to reproduce — only a single schematic equation and qualitative arguments.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No concrete predictions, formalism, or quantitative results that could connect to experiment or application.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Extraordinary claims are made with no supporting calculations, derivations, or quantitative evidence whatsoever.

title: CARD: Non-Uniform Quantization of Visual Semantic Unit for Generative Recommendation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with vector quantization, generative recommendation, and vision-language models, but the individual components are not deeply complex
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The visual semantic unit is an unconventional approach, but the performance improvements are incremental and largely expected given the additional information and better quantization

title: An Efficient Black-Box Reduction from Online Learning to Multicalibration, and a New Route to $Φ$-Regret Minimization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with online learning, Φ-regret minimization, variational inequalities, and RKHS theory; the individual proofs are elegant but the conceptual landscape is sophisticated.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The existence of a GGM-like theorem for multicalibration is not entirely unexpected given structural parallels, but the identification of EVIs as the correct primitive and the dramatic simplification of Φ-regret results are genuinely surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are fully specified with pseudocode, proofs are complete and self-contained, and all building blocks are precisely referenced. No code, but the theoretical results are fully replicable.

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The framework works for arbitrary compact convex outcome spaces, general function classes H, and extends to delayed, censored, and contextual settings; the RKHS extension covers an enormous family of deviation classes.

title: PQC-Enhanced QKD Networks: A Layered Approach

new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on linear chain topologies with simulated QKD devices; unclear how it performs with real key rate constraints, complex topologies, or production traffic

title: From Manipulation to Mistrust: Explaining Diverse Micro-Video Misinformation for Robust Debunking in the Wild

new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and data released; prompt templates fully specified; however, reliance on external APIs and retrieval infrastructure introduces variability
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to fact-checking organizations and content moderation platforms; explanation generation adds practical value
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Broad experiments across many baselines but no statistical significance tests, limited ablation depth, and human evaluation on only 200 samples

title: Few-shot Protein Fitness Prediction via In-context Learning and Test-time Training
