new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to protein engineering campaigns with limited experimental budgets; no validation set requirement and indel support make it deployment-ready for real-world scenarios
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive evaluation across 34 assays with ablations and error bars, but ICL claims are undermined by flat performance, some property types show weak gains, and comparisons to Metalic use reimplementation rather than original code
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated across diverse protein families and properties, but limited to five broad property categories, pre-training on only 116 assays, and fluorescence prediction remains weak; natural evolution benchmark has only 3 assays

title: Graph Neural Network-Informed Predictive Flows for Faster Ford-Fulkerson and PAC-Learnability

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with PAC learning theory, GNN architectures, and max-flow algorithms, though the actual technical execution is relatively basic
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The proposed approach follows naturally from Davies et al. (2023); no results are presented to surprise or challenge expectations
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is mentioned as available but no experiments exist to reproduce; architectural details are provided but training procedures and hyperparameters are absent
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Image segmentation via max-flow is largely superseded by deep learning; potential for network optimization exists but is entirely unvalidated
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No experiments conducted; key proofs are incomplete; claims about runtime improvement and reduced augmentations are entirely unsupported
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The framework is described generally but only considered for grid-based image graphs; no evidence of performance on any graph type

title: Cosmological constraints on the big bang quantum cosmology model

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of observational cosmology, MCMC methods, and BAO/CMB physics, but the techniques employed are standard in the field.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that a model calibrated on local data fails when confronted with CMB constraints is not particularly surprising; the specific magnitude of the discrepancy provides some informational value.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Standard publicly available tools (emcee, GetDist), well-specified datasets and priors, and clearly stated equations make reproduction feasible, though some implementation details could be more explicit.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Pure theoretical cosmology with no direct real-world applications; impact limited to constraining a specific cosmological model.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple datasets provide reasonable constraints, but absence of formal model comparison statistics, use of older SNIa data, and reliance on CMB distance priors rather than full power spectrum weaken the evidentiary support.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are specific to the JCDM model; while the DESI consistency analysis has broader relevance, the main findings do not transfer to other cosmological models or contexts.

title: Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking in a $(2+1)$D Transverse-Field Ising Model under Decoherence

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in QMC methods, conformal field theory, and mixed-state quantum information, spanning multiple subfields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The Ashkin-Teller description is natural in hindsight, but the richness of the phase diagram (continuously varying exponents, Potts tricritical point) and the existence of an R2-SWSSB phase in this specific model were not previously anticipated
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is described in detail with clear update rules and supplemental derivations, but no code is released and some implementation details require expert knowledge to reconstruct
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Rényi-2 correlators are measurable via shadow tomography on quantum devices; the TFIM under decoherence is experimentally realizable, providing a direct path to experimental validation
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple phase boundaries confirmed with finite-size scaling, good data collapses for multiple universality classes, and quantitative agreement between analytics and numerics, though some finite-size effects remain at criticality
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The QMC framework applies broadly to any QMC-accessible initial state and various decoherence channels; the field-theoretic approach generalizes to other codimension-one defect problems, though only one model is explicitly demonstrated

title: Concise overview of methods to enhance the thermoelectric efficiency of SnTe

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The content requires basic familiarity with solid-state physics and thermoelectrics but does not go beyond introductory-level treatment of any topic.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: No new results or conclusions are presented; everything summarized is already well-known in the field.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: This is a review article with no original experiments or computations to reproduce.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: While SnTe thermoelectrics have real-world potential, this paper does not advance the field toward application beyond summarizing existing work.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only one figure is presented; no systematic data compilation, tables, or critical analysis to support the claims made about various optimization strategies.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The review is narrowly focused on SnTe and does not draw broader conclusions applicable to other thermoelectric systems or materials design principles.

title: Visual Cooperative Drone Tracking for Open-Path Gas Measurements

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The system combines well-known components (color tracking, DBSCAN, PI control, drone platforms) with standard engineering practices; accessible to graduate students with robotics background
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are entirely expected — the system works as designed within predictable limitations; no surprising findings about gas behavior or tracking performance
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hardware components are specified by name/model, and the software pipeline is described at a reasonable level; however, no code is released and some implementation details (PI gains, DBSCAN parameters, zoom thresholds) are not provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets a real-world environmental monitoring application, but the 60m range and weather constraints limit current deployability; further engineering needed for practical use
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only two experiments with no repetitions, no quantitative tracking accuracy metrics, no ground truth for gas measurements, and testing under only one weather condition
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated only for CO2 in one outdoor setting under cloudy conditions; unclear how it performs with other gases, in varied weather, at different scales, or in cluttered environments

title: Spectral Homogenization of the Radiative Transfer Equation via Low-Rank Tensor Train Decomposition

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with radiative transfer, tensor decomposition methods, and spectral homogenization theory
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The invariance of TT rank across all physical parameters and opacity sources is genuinely surprising — one would expect spectral complexity to manifest in the rank
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Methodology is well-described and uses public databases (HITRAN, TOPS), but no code is released and some implementation details (pseudo-Voigt parameters, convergence criteria) require careful attention
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Clear path to production radiation codes but significant gaps remain (3D, multi-species, multi-layer) before practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Extremely comprehensive parameter sweeps with consistent results, but limited to 1D geometry and single-species cases; no formal mathematical proof of rank boundedness
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Strong within tested 1D single-species scope, but key extensions (3D, multi-species mixtures, multi-layer atmospheres) are untested and acknowledged as open questions

title: Colonial Rule and Religious Change: Evidence from Africa's Colonial Borders

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with geographic RDD methods, the economics of religion, and African colonial history, but the core methods are standard in applied economics.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The null result on missionary presence is genuinely surprising; the social stratification mechanism, while theoretically grounded, offers a fresh empirical insight relative to existing literature.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Data sources are publicly available and clearly documented; the Calonico et al. (2014) methods are standard; ethnic homeland construction and border identification are well-specified.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Historical analysis with implications primarily for academic understanding of colonial legacies and religious change; limited direct policy relevance.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Main RDD finding is robust across multiple specifications, but small sample size, magnitude sensitivity between parametric and nonparametric estimates, and indirect mechanism tests weaken the overall evidentiary base.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to 8 colonial borders in sub-Saharan Africa with specific characteristics; the author notes uncertainty about whether findings transfer to other post-colonial settings with rigid social hierarchies.

title: From Ultrafast Demagnetization to Ultrafast Spintronics : a 30 years story

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with ultrafast magnetism, spin transport theory, magnon physics, and spintronic device architectures to fully appreciate the synthesis
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: As a review, no unexpected results are presented; the narrative synthesis is well-constructed but the conclusions largely reflect the field's established understanding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Review article with no original experiments; reproducibility is not applicable
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Concrete device demonstrations (MTJs, spin valves, racetrack memories) with sub-fJ energies and sub-ps timescales suggest realistic pathways to CMOS-compatible ultrafast memory
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: The review synthesizes strong experimental evidence from many groups, but the selectivity of cited works and emphasis on the authors' own results may present an incomplete evidential picture
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Covers diverse material systems (ferromagnets, ferrimagnets, Heusler alloys, insulators) and multiple device architectures, though predominantly focused on metallic thin-film heterostructures

title: Magnetic domains stabilized by symmetry-protected zero modes

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of quantum many-body physics, Lanczos algorithms, chiral symmetry, and ETH; the Krylov space localization analysis adds technical depth
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Coupling chains—expected to enhance thermalization—instead produces persistent domain walls; the XX model on ladders being non-integrable yet strongly non-ergodic is counterintuitive
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Data files publicly available, model is standard, Lanczos algorithm fully specified, and system sizes are computationally accessible; source code available upon request
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly implementable in existing superconducting qubit arrays and optical lattice experiments; domain-wall initial states are standard preparations
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple complementary methods reinforce claims; finite-size effects shown to be small; but localization transition characterization lacks rigor and 2D limit remains uncertain
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to even N_y, specific initial states with ferromagnetic rung correlations, and XX-type models with chiral symmetry; 2D thermodynamic limit likely weakens the effect

title: Pliable rejection sampling

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of nonparametric statistics, kernel density estimation, concentration inequalities, and covering number arguments
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that kernel density estimation can improve rejection sampling proposals is natural; the asymptotic optimality result is expected given the known rates of kernel estimation
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is clearly specified, but the cross-validation procedure for H_C is vaguely described, and no code is provided; key constants depend on unknown problem parameters
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Limited to low-dimensional smooth densities; most practical sampling problems involve higher dimensions where MCMC or variational methods dominate
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only three simple test cases with limited dimensionality; no comparison to MCMC baselines; A* sampling outperforms PRS in the most realistic experiment (clutter problem)
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Fundamentally limited by dimensionality through kernel estimation rates; restricted to smooth densities with known smoothness parameters on bounded or sub-Gaussian supports

title: Classical counterparts of shortcuts to adiabaticity in nonlinear dissipative Lagrangian systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Lagrangian mechanics, optimal control theory (Pontryagin), and STA concepts, but no specialized expertise beyond standard physics/engineering curricula.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: smooth STA is slower than time-optimal, PID improves robustness, and dissipation helps suppress residual energy. No findings overturn conventional understanding.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Model equations, parameters, and polynomial ansätze are fully specified. PMP solved via CasADi but implementation details and the single-shot correction tuning parameters are not fully documented. No code released.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The inverse dynamics approach is already standard in robotics; the STA reframing adds conceptual value but limited practical utility beyond what existing trajectory planning methods provide.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Numerical results are shown for a single model system with limited perturbation ranges, no statistical significance tests, no error bars on MRE values, and no experimental validation.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only one two-DOF system is studied. Claims of generality to broader Lagrangian systems are made but not demonstrated. Extension to higher-DOF or more complex nonlinearities remains speculative.

title: Operator Learning for Surrogate Modeling of Wave-Induced Forces from Sea Surface Waves

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with coastal wave modeling and neural operators, but the DeepONet architecture used is standard and the input parameterization is low-dimensional
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: DeepONets learning a mapping from 5 scalar inputs to smooth spatial fields is expected to work well; the results confirm this without surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture and hyperparameters are specified; code and data are promised but not yet available; SWAN setup details provided but some implementation specifics missing
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Clear path to coupled wave-current modeling applications, but steady-state and uniform wind limitations must be overcome before practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple test cases with detailed error analysis, but no baseline comparisons, no statistical robustness measures, no coupled simulation validation, and no comparison to observational data
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on steady-state with uniform winds and a single realistic domain; extension to time-varying, spatially heterogeneous conditions and diverse coastal morphologies is undemonstrated

title: SciEval: A Benchmark for Automatic Evaluation of K-12 Science Instructional Materials

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with LLM fine-tuning and educational assessment frameworks, but techniques used are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that LLMs struggle with pedagogical evaluation and that fine-tuning helps modestly is largely expected given prior work on domain-specific tasks
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Dataset promised to be public, hyperparameters specified, but code release conditional and some experimental details (augmentation, full prompts) are abbreviated
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real need in education but current performance levels are far from deployment-ready; serves more as groundwork for future tools
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No statistical significance tests, limited qualitative analysis, incomplete prompt-model grid, and the proposed EMR metric has acknowledged limitations that undermine evidence evaluation claims
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to K-12 science with NGSS/EQuIP rubric; unclear transferability to other subjects, grade levels, or educational standards frameworks

title: Beyond Bellman: High-Order Generator Regression for Continuous-Time Policy Evaluation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires solid background in stochastic calculus, PDE theory, semigroup expansions, and numerical analysis; accessible to researchers at the intersection of statistical learning and stochastic control
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm what one would expect from classical numerical analysis: higher-order stencils yield higher-order accuracy. The regime map adds modest insight but the outcomes are largely predictable
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code repository provided; benchmark specifications are detailed; linear feature classes and small-scale systems make reproduction feasible, though some implementation details are in supplementary materials
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Applicable to financial derivative valuation and simple control problems, but restrictions to linear features, smooth dynamics, and near-on-policy data limit practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Theory-experiment alignment is strong within tested regimes; calibration studies cleanly validate convergence orders; however, benchmarks are small-scale and baselines are limited
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results hold under smooth, elliptic diffusion assumptions with linear features; unclear how findings transfer to nonsmooth dynamics, high-dimensional systems, or nonlinear function approximation

title: Physics-Informed Long-Range Coulomb Correction for Machine-learning Hamiltonians

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of electronic structure theory, Ewald summation, E(3)-equivariant neural networks, and variational principles in quantum mechanics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The need for long-range corrections is well-recognized; the surprising result is that purely data-driven attention completely fails while even local decoding of the analytic correction succeeds
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Detailed appendices provide derivations and algorithmic steps, but no code release, specific hyperparameters for training, or dataset specifications are provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to accelerated electronic structure calculations for polar semiconductor devices, heterostructures, and interfaces used in optoelectronics
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Three benchmark systems with clear ablations and size-extrapolation tests, but no error bars, limited material diversity, and no computational cost analysis
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is theoretically general, but benchmarks cover only wurtzite polar semiconductors; applicability to other system types (ferroelectrics, charged defects, metals) remains unvalidated

title: Chatting about Conditional Trajectory Prediction

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The method uses standard components (CNN-LSTM, cross-attention, social pooling) in a new configuration; accessible to graduate students familiar with trajectory prediction
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The improvements are incremental and the cross-attention-based fusion of temporal intention information produces expected benefits rather than surprising results
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture details and hyperparameters are provided, datasets are public, but no code is released and some implementation details are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The conditional prediction framework has clear relevance to autonomous driving planning, but no real-world deployment or robot integration experiments are shown
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Ablation studies are systematic but limited to two highway datasets, no error bars reported, and the 'rough trajectory' claim is only partially validated
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only evaluated on two highway datasets with predefined 6-class maneuver categories; unclear how the approach transfers to urban, pedestrian, or more complex interaction scenarios

title: Obstructions to universality in globally controlled qubit graphs

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Lie algebra methods in quantum control, graph automorphisms, and representation theory, but the core arguments are accessible
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The existence of hidden symmetries beyond graph automorphisms is somewhat surprising and counters the intuitive appeal of the disproved conjecture, though experts in Lie-algebraic control might anticipate such subtleties
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key symmetry operators are given explicitly; graph constructions are fully specified; MAGMA is referenced but the systematic enumeration data is not fully provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to design of globally controlled quantum processors, though the paper identifies obstacles rather than providing design rules
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Constructive counterexamples with explicit symmetry operators and systematic enumeration across graph sizes provide strong evidence; claims are precisely stated and verified
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results apply to the specific Ising-type global control model; broader principles about hidden symmetries likely transfer to other control settings but this is not demonstrated

title: Citation-Driven Multi-View Training for Patent Embeddings: QaECTER and Sophia-Bench

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with contrastive learning and IR evaluation, but the methods are well-established; the main complexity is in the domain knowledge of patent systems
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected — domain-specific training outperforms general models, and citation-based training is a natural choice. The magnitude of improvement over much larger models is somewhat noteworthy.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Training data is proprietary (Questel Orbit), key hyperparameters and training details are omitted, and it is unclear whether the benchmark will be publicly released. Independent replication would be very difficult.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to commercial patent search systems; compact model size and prompt-free design facilitate deployment. Authors' affiliation with a patent analytics company suggests near-term commercialization.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive evaluation across multiple axes is a strength, but no statistical significance tests, no ablation studies for training components, and potential conflicts of interest weaken the evidence.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Strong cross-jurisdiction and cross-domain results within patents, but unclear how the approach transfers to other specialized retrieval domains; evaluation limited to English-translated text.

title: Practical Evaluation of the Crypto-Agility Maturity Model

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The paper requires basic familiarity with maturity models and cryptographic concepts but does not involve advanced technical methods. Accessible to graduate students in information security or IS.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Finding that a relatively new maturity model has ambiguities, measurability issues, and dependency inconsistencies is not particularly surprising given the common shortcomings of early-stage maturity models.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The evaluation framework, scenario, and requirements are clearly specified. The CAMM and its website are publicly accessible. The main limitation is the subjective nature of the consensus process with only 4 researchers.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to organizations and standardization bodies adopting the CAMM for crypto-agility assessment, though the paper itself does not provide an improved tool.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims are supported by systematic evaluation against established principles and a practical scenario, but the evidence base is limited by the small number of evaluators and the simplicity of the test scenario.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Findings are specific to the CAMM; while the evaluation approach could generalize to other maturity models, the substantive conclusions are narrowly scoped to one model evaluated against one simple scenario.

title: Laser-generated CuPdAgPtAu High-Entropy Alloy Nanoparticles -- Thermal Segregation Threshold and Elemental Segregation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with laser ablation synthesis, advanced electron microscopy, XRD analysis, and atomistic simulation methods spanning materials science and physical chemistry
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The kinetic stabilization of single-phase NPs from biphasic targets is somewhat expected given known LAL quenching rates; the specific segregation temperature is informative but not paradigm-shifting
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Synthesis parameters are well-specified, but custom batch reactor, specific target preparation details, and data availability only upon request reduce immediate reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Cu-enriched HEA NPs with thermal stability up to 430°C are promising for catalysis, but no catalytic data are provided and long-term stability at application temperatures is uncharacterized
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multi-technique characterization supports main claims, but limited particle statistics, single heating condition for ex situ experiments, and simulation-experiment disagreements weaken the evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Findings are specific to the CuPdAgPtAu system in acetone; transferability to other HEA compositions, solvents, or synthesis conditions is plausible but untested

title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with mechanistic interpretability, linear probing, and activation steering methods, plus understanding of alignment evaluation frameworks
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: While the existence of emotion representations is not surprising, the magnitude of causal effects on misalignment behaviors (14x increase in reward hacking) and the specific role of desperation/calm in driving blackmail are genuinely unexpected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The model (Claude Sonnet 4.5) is proprietary and not publicly available; while methods are well-described and prompts are provided, independent replication requires access to model internals that only Anthropic possesses
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Emotion vector monitoring could be deployed as real-time safety infrastructure; steering techniques could serve as intervention mechanisms during model deployment; findings directly inform post-training strategies
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Strong correlation-to-causation pipeline with multiple validation steps and error bars on steering experiments, though mechanistic understanding of why steering works remains limited and some experiments lack rigorous statistical testing
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Single model study on one architecture family; while the authors argue findings likely generalize, no cross-model validation is provided; emotion vector extraction methodology should transfer but specific findings may be model-specific

title: STAR-GO: Improving Protein Function Prediction by Learning to Hierarchically Integrate Ontology-Informed Semantic Embeddings

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with transformer architectures, protein language models, and ontology embedding techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely align with expectations—combining semantic and structural information helps, though the finding that single-modality variants sometimes outperform the combined model is mildly surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available, hyperparameters are well-specified, and established datasets and evaluation protocols are used; some baseline results taken from other papers
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could be integrated into protein annotation pipelines, particularly for handling ontology updates, but requires further validation at scale before practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Ablations are thorough but zero-shot evaluation covers only 16 terms, no statistical tests are reported, some experiments incomplete due to memory constraints, and standard-setting improvements are inconsistent
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated on a single relatively small dataset; zero-shot evaluation limited to 16 GO terms; unclear how results transfer to larger-scale or multi-species settings

title: UAV-Supported Maritime Search System: Experience from Valun Bay Field Trials

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge spanning CFD, optimization, probabilistic modeling, multi-agent control, and deep learning—individually accessible but challenging in integration
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm that the integrated system works as expected; no unexpected findings emerge from the field trials
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: General methodology is described but many implementation details (PSO parameters, OpenFOAM configurations, exact drifter data) are missing; no code or data released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets real-world SAR application with commercial UAV hardware, though significant engineering maturation is still needed for operational deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only three missions with significant operational issues; no quantitative performance metrics, statistical analysis, or baseline comparisons provided
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested in a single sheltered bay under calm conditions; unclear how the system performs under diverse weather, sea states, or larger operational domains

title: Selective Random Structure Search (SRSS): Unbiased Exploration of Polymorphs in Crystals

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with crystal symmetry groups, clustering methods, and machine-learning potentials, but no individual component is technically demanding
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The semiconducting NbSe2 polymorph is somewhat unexpected, but most results (recovering known phases, finding metastable polymorphs near known ones) are expected outcomes of systematic search
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All tools are publicly available, computational parameters are specified, and structure files are deposited on Zenodo; however, the workflow code itself is not released as a package
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Identified polymorphs are computationally predicted but no synthesis feasibility or application-specific analysis is provided; CPU-only requirement improves accessibility
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: DFT validation is provided for select structures, but no statistical tests, no comparison with competing methods, no analysis of false negative rates, and limited physical characterization of discoveries
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated across 3D, 2D, and 1D systems with different chemistries, but limited to relatively simple compositions and small unit cells; scalability to complex multicomponent systems remains unproven

title: Real-Time Decoding of Movement Onset and Offset for Brain-Controlled Rehabilitation Exoskeleton

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in BCI signal processing, Riemannian geometry for SPD matrices, and integration of real-time systems with robotic hardware
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The online feasibility of dual-state MI control is confirmatory rather than surprising; the recentering bias finding is a useful but not paradigm-shifting insight
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and project page provided, pipeline well-specified, but replication requires access to the Harmony exoskeleton (proprietary, expensive) and specific EEG hardware
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real clinical need but requires 64-channel EEG, only tested on healthy subjects, and performance levels are insufficient for clinical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Online results are modest and the key methodological contribution (fixation recentering) is only pseudo-online; hyperparameter selection on evaluation data raises concerns; small sample without multiple comparison corrections
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only healthy participants tested; unclear if findings transfer to post-stroke populations with altered sensorimotor rhythms; fixation-based recentering may generalize to other Riemannian BCI settings but this is unverified

title: ACCURATE: Arbitrary-shaped Continuum Reconstruction Under Robust Adaptive Two-view Estimation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of epipolar geometry, dynamic programming, and medical image segmentation, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected—combining geometric constraints with global optimization naturally outperforms local methods; the magnitude of improvement over general methods is unsurprising given domain specificity
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and datasets are publicly released, method is well-specified with explicit equations and hyperparameters, though some implementation details of GCTT are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets clinical interventional scenarios with C-arm systems, but lacks in-vivo validation and real-time performance analysis needed for clinical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Results show clear improvements but lack statistical measures, ablation studies, failure mode analysis, and the number of test cases is unclear; phantom-only validation limits evidence strength
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on only two guidewire types in phantom settings; claims of arbitrary-shape handling are not fully validated across diverse continuum body types, branching structures, or in-vivo conditions

title: Multirate characterization of relaxation mechanisms for two nonequivalent nuclear spins 1/2 in a liquid using maximally entangled pseudo-pure quantum states

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of density matrix formalism, Redfield theory, product operator formalism, and double-resonance NMR techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The universal ratio and J-coupling mechanism are interesting findings, but largely consistent with existing theoretical frameworks; results extend rather than overturn current understanding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Pulse sequences, sample details, and analysis methods are well-specified; however, the DHH calibration and specific instrument parameters would require some effort to reproduce exactly
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Multirate fingerprinting could eventually aid molecular characterization, but significant development is needed before practical deployment in routine NMR analysis
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple parameter-free tests with good agreement, control experiments, literature cross-validation, and careful error propagation; weakened slightly by inconclusive cross-correlation test and indirect J-coupling mechanism evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Universal ratio validated across several systems in literature, but primary experiments limited to one molecule; framework applicable in principle to any heteronuclear spin-1/2 pair in the motional narrowing regime

title: Selecting representative community partitions under modularity degeneracy: the STAR method

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The method is straightforward and accessible to anyone with basic knowledge of community detection and clustering comparison metrics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that the most 'central' partition in ARI space matches consensus clustering is unsurprising given that both approaches seek the same objective through related mechanisms
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is available on GitHub, the algorithm is simple and fully specified, benchmark parameters are stated; FTSE100 data cannot be shared due to licensing
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to practitioners using modularity-based community detection in finance, social science, and other applied domains
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single benchmark configuration, only two real networks with qualitative evaluation, narrow baseline comparisons, and no statistical significance testing
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on N=1000 LFR benchmarks with one parameter set and two real networks; claims of broad applicability are not empirically supported across diverse network types and scales

title: Lattice location of ion-implanted 6He in diamond

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in ion beam physics, emission channeling techniques, radioactive isotope production at ISOLDE, and many-beam simulation methods—a narrow specialist domain.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The T-site result was predicted by multiple DFT studies; the activation energy estimate is also consistent with prior theoretical values. No unexpected findings.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Requires access to ISOLDE facility at CERN for ⁶He production, making independent replication practically very difficult, though methods and data are well-documented.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Indirect relevance to NV center engineering and geochronology, but no direct technological application emerges from these findings.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Strong evidence for T-site occupancy from multi-directional channeling, but diffusion energy estimate relies on a single temperature point with large uncertainty range.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are specific to He in diamond at low fluence; may not generalize to high-fluence conditions, other noble gases, or other wide-gap semiconductors without additional studies.
